title: [$250] [Android] Receipt capture produces mostly black/blank image

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
**Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1778675274739809 ## Description When capturing a receipt on Android, the resulting image is sometimes mostly or entirely black/blank. This has been reported by multiple users and appears to be intermittent — taking a new photo (which creates a new expense) works as a workaround. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the Expensify app on Android 2. Create a new expense and use the camera to capture a receipt 3. Observe that the captured image is mostly or entirely black **Expected:** The captured receipt image should be clear and usable. **Actual:** The image is ~90% black/blank. Retaking the photo (creating a new expense) resolves it. ## Additional Context - Reported on Android devices only so far - There is speculation this may be related to a snapshot-based capture change on Android (though the related PR https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88575 has not been deployed yet, there may be other snapshot-related changes in play) - Multiple users have experienced this issue independently

  • Upwork Job ID: 2054645997761418508
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-05-13

title: [$250] Add Custom Field 1, Custom Field 2, and International Reimbursement IDs columns

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
## Summary Add Report Created, Custom Field 1, Custom Field 2, and Reimbursement IDs columns to match what's available in the detailed export template and close the gap between in-product column configuration and exports. This is part of a larger effort to add custom columns: - https://github.com/Expensify/Expensify/issues/622813 ## Expected Behavior References to the template below are included here. - Users can display the Custom Field 1 column (custom field data): `${report.submitterUserID}` - Users can display the Custom Field 2 column (custom field data): `${report.submitterPayrollID}` - Users can display the International Reimbursement IDs column (associated reimbursement identifiers): `${report.orderDealNumbers}` - Columns follow existing Expensify column styling and behavior ## Context Currently, ~29 columns are available in-product; the detailed export template has 67. Top customers have requested the ability to configure columns in the product that match their export templates to reduce downstream Excel manipulation. Reference: `Web-Expensify/lib/Integrations/defaultExportTemplates/detailed_export.ftl` ([expensify_detailed_export.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27710929/expensify_detailed_export.txt)) for context on available columns. ## Performance Considerations - Verify no performance impact on accounts with large report histories - Monitor render times when displaying these columns alongside other data ## Testing - Verify columns display correctly with various data states - Verify columns are configurable via the column selection menu - Test on accounts with large report histories for performance impact
